1.description: Monthly structured equity product issuances and proceeds. This figure shows the monthly number of SEP issues (blue dotted line, left scale), number of unique reference stocks (red dashed line, left scale), and total proceeds in millions of dollars (solid black line, right scale) during the period running from January 2000 through December 2019. SEP issuances are identified by searching the SEC's EDGAR database, as detailed in SectionI.

2.description: Monthly proceeds of SEPs based on stocks in top three industries. This figure shows the monthly SEP proceeds in millions of dollars for the three most frequently referenced industries (Business Equipment, Energy, and Finance) during the time period running from January 2000 through December 2019.

3.description: SEP sentiment measures. Time series of the SEP sentiment measureGt,n${G_{t,n}}$during the period running from January 2004 through December 2019. Panel A reportsGt,n${G_{t,n}}$for the five stocks with the greatest average sentiment measures, subject to the restriction of having at least 60 months’ data in the sample period: Chesapeake Energy (CHK), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Under Armour (UAA), United Rentals (URI), and U.S. Steel (X). Panel B displays the time series ofGt,n${G_{t,n}}$for five stocks with average levels ofGt,n${G_{t,n}}$closest to the median: Caterpillar (CAT), General Motors (GM), Dow Chemical (DOW), Micron Tech (MU), and Schlumberger (SLB).

4.description: Realized abnormal returns. Time series of realized abnormal returns from January 2004 to December 2019. The realized abnormal return (dashed line) is the sum of the intercept and residual from the Fama-French three-factor model for which estimates are reported in TableI, Panel A. The solid line plots the 12-month moving average of the realized abnormal returns.

5.description: Daily cumulative abnormal returns, in event time, starting from the first trade date of the first postissuance month. For each issuance month, we form a portfolio using the SEP sentiment measureG, and compute the daily portfolio returns over a 121-day window centered on the last trade date of the issuance month. We use these portfolio returns, together with daily factor returns, to estimate the parameters of the Fama-French three-factor model and the associated daily abnormal returns, where for each trade date the abnormal return is the sum of the intercept and the regression residual. For each issuance month, we compute the CARs starting from first trade date in the postissuance month, and then compute the cross-sectional average CARs, in event time. The figure displays these cross-sectional average CARs for dates in the interval [1, 50], using data from the 2004 to 2019 and 2006 to 2019 samples.

6.description: Aggregate SEP proceeds and the Baker-Wurgler index. The figure plots the three-month moving average of monthly aggregate SEP proceeds (MA(Q)) along with the Baker-Wurgler sentiment indexSENT. The monthly aggregate SEP proceeds are computed as the cross-sectional sum of the proceeds of all SEP issues during the month. The sample of SEP issues begins in January 2004 so that the three-month moving average begins in March 2004. The sample ends in December 2018 because the Baker-Wurgler index was not available for 2019, when we constructed the figure.
